prose:
Computed property: Heat of formation (ΔH_f)
Level of theory: PM7 (MOPAC semi-empirical NDDO Hamiltonian; no Gaussian basis set)
Geometry provenance: User-supplied structure (ferrocene.xyz); single-point only – no geometry relaxation
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: MOPAC through chemkit backend (version as in backend; see live .out log)
Convergence & warnings: Single-point job completed; PM7 SCF converged. Warning from engine echoed verbatim below.

Result
• ΔH_f (PM7 final heat of formation) = 50.29 kcal mol⁻¹  (= 2.18 eV = 0.0801 Eh)

Caveats
• Warning from MOPAC/PM7: "PM7 has poorly validated parameters for: ['Fe']. Treat absolute energies and barriers with skepticism." Absolute heats of formation for transition-metal systems like ferrocene are known to carry significant uncertainty at the PM7 semi-empirical level; trends are generally more reliable than absolute values.
• PM7 defines its own zero of energy (elements in their standard states); do not compare this value directly to ab-initio total energies.

Reproducibility
The run was executed with the command recorded in the result JSON:
/Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py sp --method mopac /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/runs/ferrocene_sp_mopac/ferrocene.xyz --out /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmpbdvd4mw6/live.json
A live engine log was written during execution (path shown at run time; available on request) and the full result JSON is saved at the path above.
